steps.title: Rewrite the quadratic in standard form
steps.content: Multiply both sides of $$-x^2 + bx - 64 = 0$$ by $-1$ to get $$x^2 - bx + 64 = 0$$. Here $a=1$, the linear coefficient is $-b$, and the constant term is $c=64$.

steps.title: Apply the discriminant condition for no real solutions
steps.content: The discriminant is $$D = (-b)^2 - 4(1)(64) = b^2 - 256$$. For no real solutions we need $$D < 0$$, i.e. $$b^2 - 256 < 0$$ which simplifies to $$b^2 < 256$$ or $$|b| < 16$$. Hence $b$ must be an integer satisfying $-15 \le b \le 15$.

steps.title: Find the least possible integer b
steps.content: From the inequality $$|b| < 16$$ the smallest integer value is $-15$. Therefore the least possible $b$ is $-15$. The answer is -15.
